_meta.source: Extracted from Firecrawl crawl of lab news/blog pages on 2026-05-24 (re-crawl with Qwen, BFL, Moonshot added; Sakana removed)
_meta.extractor: Manual extraction in Claude Code (no API spend beyond the Max-plan session)
_meta.note: Google DeepMind items and AI2 May/April items show only month-precision on source pages; recorded as 2026-05-15 / 2026-04-15 placeholders. Moonshot /news URL returned an Nginx stub, so Moonshot items come from homepage only.

items.title: Project Glasswing: An initial update
items.summary: Progress update on the multi-vendor security initiative bringing together AWS, Apple, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Cisco, JPMorganChase and others under Anthropic coordination.
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic positioning itself as the cross-vendor convener on AI security — first time major cloud and enterprise platforms co-sign an AI safety effort. For B2B teams selling into regulated industries, expect Glasswing references in security questionnaires within 6-12 months.

items.title: OpenAI named a Leader in enterprise coding agents by Gartner
items.summary: Gartner placed OpenAI in the Leaders quadrant for enterprise coding agents.
items.why_it_matters: Gartner placement matters in enterprise procurement. If you sell coding agents or compete with Codex, expect this to show up in 'why not OpenAI' deal objections within the next sales cycle.

items.title: FLUX Erase: remove anything, leave no trace
items.summary: Removes whatever you mask — object, shadow, reflection — and reconstructs the scene behind it without touching the rest of the image.
items.why_it_matters: Photoshop-style intelligent erase as an API call. If you build image-editing products (e-commerce photo cleanup, content moderation, photo apps), this replaces complex masking + diffusion pipelines with a single endpoint.

items.title: An OpenAI model disproves a central conjecture in discrete geometry
items.summary: An OpenAI model produced an original mathematical result that disproves a previously-held conjecture in discrete geometry.
items.why_it_matters: First widely-cited example of a frontier model producing novel mathematical results that displace human-conjectured truths. Signal for any product depending on rigorous symbolic or formal reasoning — capability is real, not extrapolation.

items.title: Introducing Command A+: sovereign agentic capabilities, open-source
items.summary: Cohere's fastest, most powerful language model released open-source, framed around sovereign agentic deployments.
items.why_it_matters: Cohere doubling down on open-source for sovereign/private use cases. Notable for any enterprise procurement where sovereignty matters — Command A+ is now the most credible open-source enterprise-tier option vs. closed APIs.

items.title: KPMG integrates Claude across its core business and workforce of 276,000+
items.summary: Strategic alliance deploying Claude across KPMG's global operations and full 276,000-person workforce.
items.why_it_matters: Deployment at this scale at a Big Four firm signals that frontier LLMs have crossed the procurement threshold in top professional services. If you sell AI features into finance/audit/consulting, Claude is now the assumed baseline buyers compare against.

items.title: Advancing content provenance for a safer AI ecosystem
items.summary: New work from OpenAI on content provenance technology for transparent AI-generated content.
items.why_it_matters: Provenance and watermarking are becoming table stakes. If your product generates text, images, or video, expect platform and regulatory requirements around provenance within 12 months — this is the reference implementation everyone else will be measured against.

items.title: Cohere acquires Reliant AI for biopharma and healthcare
items.summary: Cohere acquired Reliant AI to expand sovereign enterprise AI for the global biopharma and healthcare sectors.
items.why_it_matters: Vertical AI consolidation in regulated industries is accelerating. If you compete in life-sciences AI, Cohere just got harder to displace; if you build adjacent tools, the integration surface gets bigger.

items.title: Qwen3.7-Max: built for the agent era
items.summary: Qwen3.7-Max is built as an agent foundation — writing and debugging code, automating office workflows, and sustaining autonomous execution across hundreds or thousands of steps.
items.why_it_matters: Major Chinese frontier model targeting agent workloads specifically. If you build coding or autonomous agents, Qwen 3.7-Max is now in the benchmark mix alongside Claude/GPT — particularly relevant for cost-sensitive agent deployments where DeepSeek and Qwen pricing dominate.

items.title: Qwen3.5-LiveTranslate-Flash: multimodal simultaneous interpretation
items.summary: Real-time multimodal translation built on Qwen3.5-Omni — hears and translates speech and also sees and understands visual context for more accurate output.
items.why_it_matters: Voice + visual context translation is niche but rising — useful for live interpretation, accessibility, multilingual video. Open-weight option in a category dominated by closed APIs (Google Translate, Meta SeamlessM4T).

items.title: OpenAI and Dell partner to bring Codex on-premises
items.summary: OpenAI and Dell Technologies partnership to bring Codex to hybrid and on-premises enterprise environments.
items.why_it_matters: On-prem is OpenAI's wedge into Fortune 500s that can't send code to a SaaS. If you sell coding tools to regulated enterprises, expect Codex on-prem to surface in 'why not OpenAI?' procurement conversations.

items.title: A new personal finance experience in ChatGPT
items.summary: Native personal finance features built into ChatGPT.
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI continues to absorb verticals into ChatGPT itself. If your product is a personal finance or budgeting assistant, this is the existential question for your category — built-in default vs. standalone app.

items.title: Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action
items.summary: Latest Gemini series combining frontier reasoning with action and tool-use capabilities, deployed across Google's product surfaces.
items.why_it_matters: Gemini 3.5 is the new ground floor for Google's billion-user surfaces (Search, Workspace, Android). For products competing for the same eyeballs, this is the free default users compare against.

items.title: Introducing Gemini Omni
items.summary: Multimodal model that creates 'anything from anything, starting with video' — accepts and generates across text, audio, image, and video.
items.why_it_matters: First-party multimodal in/out at frontier quality. If your product workflow involves video transcription, editing, or generation, the bar for what consumers expect just moved decisively.

items.title: Co-Scientist: multi-agent AI partner to accelerate research
items.summary: Multi-agent AI system designed to collaborate with human researchers on scientific discovery.
items.why_it_matters: Pushes 'AI as research collaborator' from demo to deployable. Relevant for biotech, materials, and climate startups — Google is offering this as infrastructure, not just a paper, which means productized versions hit your customers' workflows next.

items.title: Introducing Google Antigravity 2.0
items.summary: Updated version of Google's AI-native coding agent / development environment.
items.why_it_matters: Google's direct competitor to Cursor and Claude Code. If you build dev tooling, Antigravity 2.0 is now the Google-stack default — and the third frontier-lab coding agent any IDE-vendor pitch will be benchmarked against.

items.title: Gemma 4 open-weight model release
items.summary: DeepMind's most intelligent open-weight model family, built to maximize intelligence-per-parameter.
items.why_it_matters: Gemma 4 raises the open-weight floor again. If you self-host, fine-tune, or deploy to edge, recheck whether your current Llama/Mistral base is still the right pick.

items.title: MolmoAct 2: open foundation for robots in the real world
items.summary: Fully open robotics foundation model with faster, stronger 3D action reasoning designed for real-world robot deployment.
items.why_it_matters: Fully-open robotics foundation models are rare — most are closed (NVIDIA Isaac, Figure, Tesla Optimus). MolmoAct 2 gives robotics startups a credible OSS base to fine-tune from, similar to what Llama did for LLMs.

items.title: AI2 brings NSF OMAI compute online for open AI research
items.summary: NSF Open Multimodal AI Infrastructure (OMAI) compute now live, powering a fully open AI research ecosystem.
items.why_it_matters: Significant academic compute resource for OSS AI research. Indirectly matters for anyone whose roadmap rides on open-source model improvements — more shared compute = faster open-source progress.

items.title: Artificial Analysis adopts AI2's IFBench instruction-following eval
items.summary: Artificial Analysis uses AI2's open IFBench eval because it captures a stubborn, real-world capability many other benchmarks miss.
items.why_it_matters: Instruction-following is harder to measure than people think. IFBench is now a reference benchmark — if you evaluate LLM quality for your product, add it to your eval suite or you're flying blind on real-world failure modes.

items.title: Helping ChatGPT better recognize context in sensitive conversations
items.summary: Improvements to how ChatGPT recognizes and handles sensitive conversations (mental health, crisis, etc.).
items.why_it_matters: Sensitive-conversation handling is the new safety frontier post-jailbreaks. If you build consumer-facing chat, what OpenAI does here sets the policy expectation regulators will eventually apply to everyone.

items.title: FLUX Outpainting: extend any image, in any direction
items.summary: Extends images beyond their original frame in any direction, preserving lighting, texture, and composition without visible seams.
items.why_it_matters: Outpainting was a manual workflow; BFL makes it a single API call. Useful for any product that needs to reformat images across aspect ratios (mobile vs desktop, ad creatives, social formats).

items.title: Building a safe, effective sandbox for Codex on Windows
items.summary: OpenAI shipped sandboxed Codex execution on Windows.
items.why_it_matters: Windows is the unsexy majority of enterprise development. Codex Windows support closes a real gap vs. Claude Code, which has been Windows-friendly from day one.

items.title: Higher usage limits for Claude and a compute deal with SpaceX
items.summary: Increased usage limits across Claude tiers alongside a major compute infrastructure partnership with SpaceX.
items.why_it_matters: Compute supply remains the real bottleneck. Deals like this translate directly to user-facing latency, rate limits, and pricing. If you're API-dependent at scale, capacity announcements are leading indicators of your own product's reliability.

items.title: Qwen-Scope: interpretability toolkit for Qwen3 and Qwen3.5
items.summary: Interpretability toolkit trained on Qwen3 and Qwen3.5 — Sparse Autoencoders inserted and trained within Qwen's hidden layers.
items.why_it_matters: Open interpretability tooling for a major open-weight model family — rare to ship this publicly. Useful if you fine-tune or audit Qwen models for safety/compliance.

items.title: Remote agents in Vibe, powered by Mistral Medium 3.5
items.summary: Mistral Medium 3.5 launches alongside remote coding agents in Vibe and a new Work mode in Le Chat for complex tasks.
items.why_it_matters: Vibe is now remote — competing with Cursor's background-agent push and Claude Code's headless mode. EU-headquartered alternative if your customers care about jurisdiction.

items.title: Workflows for work that runs the business
items.summary: Mistral Workflows, a system for building business-critical AI orchestrations, launches in public preview.
items.why_it_matters: Mistral entering the orchestration-layer market (vs. n8n, Zapier AI, OpenAI's Apps). EU-headquartered alternative for regulated industries that can't standardize on US workflow vendors.

items.title: FlashQLA: fused linear attention kernels for Gated Delta Network
items.summary: CP-/Bwd-friendly fused linear attention kernels for Gated Delta Network (GDN), the attention layer powering Qwen3-Next through Qwen3.5/3.6.
items.why_it_matters: Infrastructure-layer optimization from the Qwen team. Relevant if you self-host GDN/MoE-architecture open models and care about throughput — continues the trend of cost-cutting infra being published as research.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 (V4-Pro and V4-Flash) released
items.summary: DeepSeek-V4 ships as Pro and Flash variants, accessible via both OpenAI-compatible ChatCompletions and Anthropic-compatible Messages interfaces.
items.why_it_matters: DeepSeek explicitly mimicking both OpenAI and Anthropic API shapes — drop-in replacement for most apps. Continues cost pressure on US frontier APIs; recheck unit economics if you're API-spending at any meaningful volume.

items.title: Cohere and Aleph Alpha join forces to form global AI powerhouse
items.summary: Cohere and Germany's Aleph Alpha merge to address national/enterprise demand for sovereign AI.
items.why_it_matters: Major consolidation in EU enterprise AI. Aleph Alpha was Germany's national AI champion; combining with Cohere creates a North America + EU sovereign-focused alternative to OpenAI and Anthropic that procurement teams can actually defend.

items.title: Production-Ready W4A8: vLLM Integration and Quality Recovery
items.summary: Cohere details production-ready 4-bit weight, 8-bit activation quantization in vLLM with techniques to recover quality.
items.why_it_matters: Inference cost optimization is now a published-research-level competitive area. If you self-host, W4A8 plus recovery techniques can cut your inference bill 2-4x — directly relevant to unit economics for any high-volume LLM product.

items.title: Kimi K2.6 release
items.summary: Natively multimodal model with strong coding and agent performance across multiple modes.
items.why_it_matters: Moonshot's flagship — Kimi K2.6 is a credible alternative to DeepSeek-V4 and Claude/GPT, particularly in Chinese-market deployments and cost-sensitive agent workloads. Recheck if you've benchmarked Kimi K2 or earlier.

items.title: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
items.summary: New Anthropic Labs product letting you collaborate with Claude to create polished visual work — designs, prototypes, slides, one-pagers.
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic moving up the stack into design tooling — directly competes with Figma AI features and tools like Galileo. If your product touches visual design output, the bar just moved and the distribution channel is built into Claude.

items.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
items.summary: Latest Opus model with stronger performance across coding, agents, vision, and multi-step tasks, with greater thoroughness and consistency.
items.why_it_matters: Opus is Anthropic's flagship tier — any benchmark or pricing comparison built on Opus 4.5/4.6 needs to be re-run. Particularly relevant for coding and long-horizon agentic workloads where Opus quality drives ROI.

items.title: Built-in and custom MCPs in Mistral Studio
items.summary: Mistral Studio supports built-in and custom MCP connectors with direct tool calling and human-in-the-loop approval.
items.why_it_matters: MCP cross-vendor adoption continues — now in Mistral's hosted studio alongside Anthropic and others. The ecosystem is genuinely becoming portable, which lowers lock-in risk if you build agentic apps.

items.title: MolmoPoint and MolmoWeb: visual understanding to visual action
items.summary: Extensions of the Molmo family from visual understanding to visual action — for pointing at and acting on web interfaces.
items.why_it_matters: Web-acting and pointing models are the building blocks for any browser-control agent. AI2's open releases give a public alternative to closed offerings like Operator and Claude computer-use.

items.title: Evaluating agents for scientific discovery (ScienceWorld, DiscoveryWorld)
items.summary: Two AI2 benchmarks (ScienceWorld and DiscoveryWorld) reveal that even strong AI science agents have major capability gaps.
items.why_it_matters: Cold-water reality check on AI-for-science hype. Useful primary source for understanding where automated research actually works and where it doesn't — relevant before committing roadmap to AI-driven R&D features.

items.title: Reka Edge now available on OpenRouter
items.summary: Reka Edge models accessible via OpenRouter for zero-code-change model switching.
items.why_it_matters: OpenRouter distribution = instant access for the developer audience that routes through it. Reduces friction to A/B-test Reka against incumbents in production apps without a separate vendor onboarding.

items.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
items.summary: Meta's new research direction on 'personal superintelligence' — scaling models to individual context and preferences.
items.why_it_matters: Meta framing the AI race as 'personal' rather than 'general' superintelligence — strategic positioning that diverges from OpenAI/Anthropic AGI narratives. If your product is consumer-facing, this signals where Meta will push competitive features next.

items.title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
items.summary: Meta details its infrastructure and process for building and evaluating its largest models.
items.why_it_matters: Rare engineering-process disclosure from a frontier lab. Useful primary source for anyone building large-scale eval or training infrastructure — the cost/quality patterns Meta publishes here become your benchmark.

items.title: AutoDiscovery: from dataset to discovery (AstaLabs)
items.summary: AutoDiscovery autonomously generates hypotheses, runs experiments, and uses Bayesian surprise to chase findings most likely to be genuine discoveries — compressing weeks of analysis into hours. Free through June 2026.
items.why_it_matters: First serious commercial deployment of automated scientific discovery. Available free through June 2026 — worth trying hands-on if your team does any exploratory data analysis, especially in biotech, climate, or scientific R&D domains.

items.title: SAM 3.1: faster real-time video detection and tracking
items.summary: Segment Anything 3.1 with multiplexing and global reasoning for faster, more accessible real-time video detection and tracking.
items.why_it_matters: SAM is the de facto open segmentation model. 3.1 makes real-time video viable, which unlocks products in surveillance, sports analysis, AR, and robotics — anywhere you needed cheap per-frame segmentation that wasn't tractable before.

items.title: Introducing Cohere Transcribe (open-source speech recognition)
items.summary: Open-source speech recognition model claiming state-of-the-art performance.
items.why_it_matters: Open-source ASR has been Whisper-only for years. A credible newer challenger matters for any voice/transcription product — particularly if you need permissive licensing for commercial use without OpenAI dependency.

items.title: Voxtral TTS: open-weights text-to-speech
items.summary: Frontier open-weights text-to-speech model that is fast, instantly adaptable, and produces lifelike speech for voice agents.
items.why_it_matters: Voice agents need credible OSS TTS — Voxtral fills a real gap vs. closed options (ElevenLabs, OpenAI TTS). If you build voice products, this opens a self-host path that wasn't viable a year ago.

items.title: What 81,000 people want from AI
items.summary: Anthropic surveyed nearly 81,000 Claude.ai users on usage, hopes, and fears — largest multilingual qualitative study of its kind.
items.why_it_matters: Rare published data on real consumer AI usage at scale. Useful primary source for product positioning — ground your PRDs in actual user behavior rather than the usual founder-intuition guesses.

items.title: Introducing Forge: enterprise frontier-grade models
items.summary: Forge lets enterprises build frontier-grade AI models grounded in their proprietary knowledge.
items.why_it_matters: Mistral's bet on private/sovereign model creation for enterprises. Relevant for any operator that wants competitive advantage from proprietary data without sending it to OpenAI/Anthropic.

items.title: Leanstral: open-source code agent for Lean 4
items.summary: First open-source code agent for the Lean 4 formal proof system.
items.why_it_matters: Formal verification + LLMs is niche but growing. If you build for finance, security, or any domain needing provable correctness, Leanstral is the first credible OSS option you can self-host and audit.

items.title: Reka Edge: frontier-level edge intelligence
items.summary: Frontier-level edge intelligence model for physical AI (robotics, on-device, sensors).
items.why_it_matters: Edge LLM that's actually competitive in quality terms — relevant for robotics, IoT, automotive. If your product runs in latency-sensitive or air-gapped environments, this is a serious self-host option.

items.title: Four MTIA chips in two years: scaling AI for billions
items.summary: Meta details its custom inference chip lineup, having shipped four MTIA variants in two years.
items.why_it_matters: Hyperscalers building custom silicon at this cadence is the real moat conversation. Meta's inference economics drop faster than competitors renting NVIDIA — relevant to anyone watching cloud GPU pricing or making build-vs-buy infra calls.

items.title: Canopy Height Maps v2 (World Resources Institute)
items.summary: Open model and world-scale maps for forest canopy height, in partnership with World Resources Institute.
items.why_it_matters: Demonstrates Meta's OSS strategy beyond LLMs — useful if you build climate, agriculture, or satellite-imagery products. Open datasets at this scale are rare and hard to reproduce.

items.title: Capable, Open, and Safe: combating AI misuse
items.summary: BFL shares early results validating efforts to mitigate misuse risks of their popular FLUX visual generation models.
items.why_it_matters: Open-weight image generation + misuse mitigation is a real policy tension. BFL publishing safety results matters for any product downstream of FLUX (most major image-gen products) — sets the bar for what 'responsible open' looks like in the visual domain.

items.title: Reka n8n Community Node
items.summary: Drag-and-drop n8n node for AI-powered video and image analysis using Reka.
items.why_it_matters: n8n is widely used for low-code automation. Native Reka integration brings multimodal analysis to non-engineering teams running content workflows — expands Reka's distribution into ops/marketing audiences.

items.title: Cohere Labs launches Tiny Aya (multilingual AI accessible)
items.summary: Smaller multilingual model in the Aya family, making multilingual AI more accessible for resource-constrained deployments.
items.why_it_matters: Small multilingual models for mobile, edge, low-resource-language scenarios. If you build for non-English markets, Tiny Aya is worth benchmarking against your current default.

items.title: Agent Swarm: multi-agent coordination research
items.summary: Moonshot research on multi-agent swarm coordination architectures for complex task execution.
items.why_it_matters: Multi-agent coordination is the next frontier after single-agent reasoning. Moonshot joins the broader push toward agent-of-agents architectures — worth tracking if your roadmap involves complex agent orchestration.

items.title: Claude is a space to think (no ads commitment)
items.summary: Anthropic commits Claude will remain ad-free, explaining why advertising incentives are incompatible with a genuinely helpful AI assistant.
items.why_it_matters: Anti-ad positioning creates a clear strategic gap vs. OpenAI/Google, who haven't ruled it out. If your product depends on assistant trust (legal, medical, finance), this commitment matters for procurement and end-user perception.

items.title: WorldVQA visual question answering benchmark
items.summary: New visual question answering benchmark from the Moonshot/Kimi team.
items.why_it_matters: VQA benchmarks from frontier labs shape what 'good multimodal' means in the field. Useful if you build or evaluate multimodal products — add it to your eval mix if you serve image-understanding workflows.

items.title: FLUX.2 [klein]: towards interactive visual intelligence
items.summary: BFL's fastest FLUX models. Unified generation and editing in under a second, on hardware you already own. Apache 2.0 license on the 4B variant.
items.why_it_matters: Sub-second image generation on consumer hardware + Apache 2.0 = the open-source image-gen story for consumer apps and small-team deployments. If you self-host image gen, recheck whether FLUX.2 [klein] beats your current stack.

items.title: Introducing Rerank 4
items.summary: Cohere's most powerful reranker model yet.
items.why_it_matters: Reranking is a small but critical step in RAG. Cohere Rerank has been the default for serious RAG; v4 quality matters if you're chasing the last 5-10% of retrieval precision.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3.2 with thinking/non-thinking modes
items.summary: Both deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner upgraded to V3.2 with unified thinking and non-thinking modes.
items.why_it_matters: Single model with toggleable reasoning — same architectural bet OpenAI later made with GPT-5. Operationally simpler than maintaining a chat-vs-reasoner split in your code.

items.title: FLUX.2: frontier visual intelligence
items.summary: Latest flagship FLUX model — production-grade image generation and editing with 4MP photorealistic output and multi-reference control.
items.why_it_matters: FLUX.2 is the new BFL flagship and has distribution via Mistral, Microsoft, Adobe, Picsart, OpenArt. If you sell image-generation features, your quality benchmark and competitive moat just moved.

items.title: FLUX.2 Technical Report: analyzing and enhancing the latent space
items.summary: Technical report on analyzing and enhancing FLUX's latent space, published alongside the FLUX.2 release.
items.why_it_matters: Rare detailed technical disclosure from a frontier image-gen lab. If you do research on diffusion models or fine-tune FLUX variants, this is the canonical reference for the architecture changes.

items.title: Reka Vision sets new standard for smart home and security AI
items.summary: Reka Vision targets smart home and security AI use cases.
items.why_it_matters: Smart home security is high-volume, low-margin and needs cheap multimodal inference. Reka going after this segment could pressure competitors using GPT-4V or Claude vision pricing.

items.title: Reka joins Oracle's defense ecosystem
items.summary: Reka joins Oracle's defense ecosystem to advance multimodal AI for mission readiness.
items.why_it_matters: Defense procurement is the canary for sovereign/restricted AI. Reka in Oracle's defense stack signals where multimodal will land in gov/defense workflows over the next 18 months.

items.title: Reka Speech: high-throughput transcription and translation
items.summary: High-throughput speech transcription and translation model with native timestamp support.
items.why_it_matters: Whisper-class speech model with native timestamps and translation. Useful for any product touching meeting transcription, captioning, or multilingual content pipelines.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3.1 with hybrid reasoning architecture
items.summary: DeepSeek-V3.1 with hybrid reasoning architecture (single model with thinking and non-thinking modes), SWE-bench Verified 66.0.
items.why_it_matters: First production-grade hybrid reasoning model — established the pattern frontier labs now copy. SWE-bench Verified 66.0 makes it credibly competitive for coding agents at fractional cost.

items.title: Reka Research: knowledge made accessible
items.summary: Reka's deep-research / web-augmented research product launches.
items.why_it_matters: Reka competing with OpenAI Deep Research, Perplexity, and Anthropic's research mode. 'Research agent' is becoming a standard SaaS feature — if you sell research tools, you have multiple frontier competitors now, not zero.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1-0528 reasoning model upgrade
items.summary: Major DeepSeek-R1 upgrade with benchmark gains: AIME 2025 70.0 → 87.5, GPQA 71.5 → 81.0, reduced hallucinations and added JSON/function-calling support.
items.why_it_matters: AIME and GPQA at these levels match closed frontier models. If your product uses reasoning for math/science workflows, R1-0528 is a serious cost-vs-quality option to A/B against your current default.
